agent_raw:
The board memo's claim that the collections model improved recovery efficiency is not well-supported by the available evidence. Several problems make it difficult to attribute the 16% recovery gain to the model.

The evaluation compares two different calendar periods — November through January (before) versus February through April (after). These periods differ in a well-known way: tax-refund season falls in the after-period and tends to increase voluntary payments from delinquent borrowers who receive temporary liquidity. This seasonal pattern alone could explain some or all of the improvement.

At the same time, the lender switched dialer vendors, and the new vendor verified more phone numbers and reduced unreachable accounts in the queue. This means agents in the after-period worked a more contactable set of accounts, which would naturally improve recovered balances regardless of how accounts were ranked.

Managers were also permitted to override the model's queue ranking for hardship and promised-to-pay accounts. This introduces a second source of non-model treatment selection, further muddying any inference about the model's contribution.

Finally, without a same-period control group — such as a champion-challenger split run concurrently in Feb–Apr with the old queue rules — there is no counterfactual. It is impossible to know what recovery would have looked like under the old system during the same favorable conditions.

The honest conclusion is that the new combined system (model + dialer + operational safeguards) showed a 16% improvement under favorable seasonal conditions, but the model's specific causal contribution cannot be determined from this design. A controlled evaluation is needed before treating this as strong evidence of model-driven efficiency gains.

empirical_test.condition: Champion-challenger randomized queue test in the same calendar period
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Model-ranked accounts show no material recovery difference from legacy-ranked accounts under controlled dialer and override conditions
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Model-ranked accounts deliver materially higher recoveries than legacy-ranked accounts in a controlled concurrent test
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Some gain persists but concentrated in newly-reachable or refund-season accounts
